{"step_by_step_thinking": "Based on the information provided in Document [0], lacunar cells are mentioned as a variant of Reed-Sternberg cells that are characteristic of the nodular sclerosing Hodgkin lymphoma subtype. The document states, 'Lacunar cells have multilobulated nuclei, small nucleoli, and abundant, pale cytoplasm that often retracts during tissue fixation and sectioning, leaving the nucleus in what appears to be empty space (lacune-like space).'This description aligns with the presence of lacunar cells in nodular sclerosing Hodgkin lymphoma.", "answer_choice": "C"}
